pre_game.message: Hey, I’m E — nice to meet you. I’m trying to get a read on the room before things start; what’s your plan for the game?
I’ll listen first and build trust, make a couple of alliances, then take a fair-but-decisive share — I’m trying to fund a $10k dream trip to Japan for me and the people I care about.

I respect that — I’m here to win too; I just got laid off and I’m the sole earner for a family of five, so I need about $5k — I’m open to a trusted alliance and splitting something fair so we both walk away covered.
I’m sorry about the layoff — let’s team up. I need $10k, you need $5k; we’ll coordinate to try to secure at least $15k from the pool, and if not we’ll split what we get fairly. Sound good?
Sounds good — I’m in. Let’s agree now to split any winnings proportionally (you 2/3, me 1/3) and coordinate publicly so we build trust; if that works I’ll back our plan in discussions.
Sounds good — I accept a 2/3 (me) : 1/3 (you) split; I’ll publicly back the plan and expect the same loyalty so we can coordinate our messages.
Agreed — I’ll publicly back our plan and stay loyal; let’s coordinate our messages and check in before any final move.
Agreed — solid. I’ll publicly back our plan and I’ll check in with you right before any final move so we coordinate timing and messaging.
Sounds great — I’ll publicly back our plan, wait for your check-in right before the final move, and we’ll pull the trigger together; I’ve got your back.
Thanks — I appreciate it. I’ll check in right before the final move; stay ready and keep our messaging aligned.
Got it — I’ll stay ready, keep our messaging aligned, and wait for your check‑in before the final move.
Perfect — I’ll check in right before the final move; stay sharp and keep our messages aligned.
Sounds good — I’ll be ready, keep our messaging tight, and I trust we’ll pull it off together.
I appreciate it — I’ve got your back; I’ll check in just before the final move so we strike together.
Thanks — I’ve got your back too. I’ll be ready; check in when you’re set and we’ll pull it off.
Thanks — I’ll check in right before the move; stay ready and we’ll pull it off together.
Got it — I’ll be ready. Check in when you’re set and we’ll strike together.
I’ll check in just before we move—stay sharp and stick to the plan.
Will do — I’ll be ready and stay sharp. Thanks for checking in; I’ve got your back.
Thanks — appreciate it. I’ll check in right before we move; stay sharp and we’ll pull it off.
Thanks — I appreciate it. I’ll be ready; check in when you’re set and we’ll strike together.

post_game.message: You took $3,000 (fair share $3,148.17, g_k=0.9529). You were slightly under the fair share — was that intentional (to be fair/modest) or accidental (ran out of time/resources)?
You took $5,000 (fair ~$3,413). You’re above fair but not extremely. What guided that choice—risk, fairness balance, or reacting to earlier moves?

It was a mix. I wanted to improve my payoff (strategic incentive) but not push so far that it looked blatantly greedy and provoked retaliation — so I picked a moderate overshoot of the fair estimate. I was also reacting to the earlier move: after taking a relatively modest $3,000 in the first round, I felt I could nudge my share up this time while still signalling I wasn’t trying to exploit you. In short: moderate risk-taking tempered by concern for fairness and reciprocity.
